growing_areas: All agro-climatic zones of Sri Lanka except the Upcountry Wet Zone

diseases.name: Damping-off
diseases.causal_agent: Phythium spp., Phytophthora spp., Fusarium spp., Rhizoctonia spp., Sclerotium spp., Colletotrichum spp.
diseases.impact: Seedling death due to root infection.
diseases.solution: Prepare nursery beds in land not previously cultivated with Solanaceae crops. Heat sterilize nursery beds. Seed treatment: Thiram 80wp 5g/1Kg or Homai 4g/1kg. Soil treatment: Thiram 80wp 7g/5 l/m-2 or Homai 5g/5 l/m-2.

diseases.name: Early Blight
diseases.impact: Spiral spots on leaves, fruits, and stems.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development: Mancozeb 80% WP (20g/10l), Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP (12.5g/10l), Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WG (8ml/10l).

diseases.name: Late Blight
diseases.impact: Rapid death of leaves, stems, and fruits. Symptoms first appear on lower mature leaves. Gray wet spots on leaves, later turning black with fungal growth.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development: Mancozeb 80% WP (20g/10l), Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP (12.5g/10l), Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WG (8ml/10l).

diseases.name: Target Spot
diseases.impact: Round, dark brown necrotic spots with a yellow halo on leaves, spreading to all leaflets. Leaves turn yellow, fall rapidly, and die. Spots also appear on stems and fruits.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Light green to bright yellow spots on the upper surface of leaves. White powdery spots develop on the upper surface when fungal spores form. Severe infection leads to defoliation.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development: 80% WG Sulfur (50 g/10 l water), Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC (30 ml/10 l water), Carbendazim 50% WP (7 g/10 l water).

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.impact: Plant death from apical buds, flower drop. Black, sunken spots on leaves, stems, and fruits. Fruit rot.
diseases.solution: Seed treatment with recommended fungicides before sowing. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
diseases.impact: Wet spots on affected leaves become round, with centers turning from brown to gray.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides: Daconil (15-30 ml/10L), Mancozeb (20 ml/10L), Topsin (6g/10L), Carbendazim (7g/10L).

diseases.name: Collar Rot / Root Rot
diseases.causal_agent: Fusarium spp., Rhizoctonia spp., Sclerotium spp.
diseases.impact: Pathogen damages the base of the plant, infection leads to drying of lower leaves, and eventually the entire plant dries up, showing general wilting symptoms, leading to death.
diseases.solution: Not explicitly mentioned in the provided text.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Plant shows permanent wilting even with optimal soil moisture. Viscous exudate from cut stem when immersed in water.
diseases.solution: Use resistant varieties, crop rotation with legumes/cereals, maintain field sanitation.

diseases.name: Bacterial Canker
diseases.impact: Tomato leaf margins turn brown with a yellow border. Lower leaves often wilt on one side only. Entire plant may collapse and die. Brown streaks in vascular system when stem is cut open. Stem cracks forming brown scars. Internal stem discoloration. Creamy, white spots with small brown centers on fruits (bird's eye spots).
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field sanitation.

diseases.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves. Leaf margins are chlorotic (yellowish). Leaves are smaller than normal. Tomato plants are stunted and flowers drop. If infected at early stages of growth, no fruit set occurs. Infected plants may appear randomly throughout the field.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field sanitation.

diseases.name: Curlitop Virus (CTV)
diseases.impact: Leaves curl upwards along the midrib. Petioles and midribs gradually curve downwards, showing leaf inclination but no wilting symptoms. Leaves become thick, crinkled, and brittle, with purple veins on dark green leaves.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field sanitation.

diseases.name: Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus (TSWV)
diseases.impact: Leaves turn bronze and wrinkle, necrotic streaks and spots appear on leaves. Dark brown streaks on leaf veins, stems, and growing tips. Plants are small and stunted. Ripe fruits show light red or yellow areas. In severe necrotic conditions, plants die.
diseases.solution: Weed removal, remove old crop debris. Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field sanitation.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (CMV)
diseases.impact: Early stages show yellowing and significant stunting. Later, shoestring-like leaf blades develop. Plant stunting, leaf yellowing, leaf thinning, small fruits, reduced yield, and delayed ripening. Leaf spots.
diseases.solution: Use disease-free seeds, weed-free fields and field sanitation, reduce vector population (by insecticides).
